title: Stack registry + knowledge index + selector + context resolver, wired into the finder (fixes G3)
description: Turn the eight authored skill files into live, retrieval-grounded audit context. Extend the stack map from 3 to 7 stacks + a generic floor, build the per-process memoised SecurityKnowledgeIndex (ADR-7), the top-K SecuritySignatureSelector, and replace the head-excerpt resolveStackSecurityContext with one that renders retrieved signatures into a never-empty prompt fragment. Wire it into runSecurityAudit so the finder receives real per-stack signatures. This closes G3 (unknown/anchor/react no longer inject frontmatter filler; unknown degrades to the generic floor).

successCriteria.description: SecurityStackRegistry.resolve maps every declared stack keyword to one of the 7 SecurityStackIds and its bober.security-<stack> skill name, extending STACK_SKILL_MAP; an unknown/absent/null stack resolves to stackId 'generic' (never null, never a throw); a per-stack test asserts all 7 stacks + generic resolve to a real skill name.

SecurityKnowledgeIndex.load() parses all skills/bober.security-*/SKILL.md once and memoises; forStack(stackId) returns that stack's signatures (missing skill => [], never throws); all() returns the union; a skillsRoot option supports test injection. A test asserts each of the 8 stacks indexes to >= 6 signatures from the real files.

SecuritySignatureSelector.select({stackId, changedPaths, diffKeywords, topK}) returns at most topK signatures ranked by stack membership + keyword/path overlap, ALWAYS including the generic-floor signatures; pure and total; a test shows a diff whose keywords match a specific signature ranks it into the selection and that generic-floor signatures are always present.

resolveStackSecurityContext (replacing stack-knowledge.ts:185) returns StackSecurityContext {stackId, stackLabel, skillName, taxonomy, signatures, promptFragment} where promptFragment is NEVER empty and renders the retrieved signatures (id/title/invariant/unsafe/safe) plus the optional threatModelText; a test asserts the fragment for each of the 7 stacks + generic is non-empty and contains a stack-appropriate signature (closes G3 head-excerpt fallback).

runSecurityAudit builds its stack context via the new resolver+index+selector (fed the estimated-files paths as changedPaths for now, since the git diff provider lands in sprint 6), and an end-to-end audit test (stubbed agentic loop) shows the retrieved signatures reach the finder user message; the fail-closed parse and read-only curator toolset are unchanged.

A config omitting security is byte-identical; npm run build + typecheck + lint pass; full suite green including the existing security-auditor-agent tests (adjusted only where the resolver signature legitimately changed).

nonGoals: Do not build the git diff provider yet (sprint 6) — changedPaths come from estimatedFiles this sprint.
Do not add supply-chain scanners or the verifier (sprints 7-8).
Do not add index cache invalidation (ADR-7: per-process memoised, no runtime invalidation).
Do not change deriveVerdict or the gate.
stopConditions: All 7 stacks + generic resolve to a real, non-empty signature fragment (G3 closed).
Retrieved signatures verifiably reach the finder prompt; existing fail-closed/read-only behavior intact.
definitionOfDone: The registry, memoised index, top-K selector, and never-empty context resolver exist, are wired into runSecurityAudit, and demonstrably feed real per-stack signatures to the finder, closing G3, with all invariants preserved and the suite green.
assumptions: The old resolveStackSecurityContext/extractSecurityExcerpt/STACK_SKILL_MAP at stack-knowledge.ts:49-212 are replaced/augmented; callers of the old resolver (security-auditor-agent.ts:79) are updated to the new signature.

The eval-lenses.ts:4 LENS_CATALOG data-module pattern is the precedent for the index shape; readSkillSecurityExcerpt's never-throw fs discipline (stack-knowledge.ts:156-167) is the precedent for the index loader.
topK default is a small number (~6-8) tunable via config in a later step; the generic floor is always included.
outOfScope: git diff provider, supply-chain, verifier, benchmark, dogfood (later).
generatorNotes: Build under src/orchestrator/security-knowledge/. (1) registry.ts: SECURITY_STACK_MAP extends the precedence logic of detectStack (stack-knowledge.ts:64-98) — blockchain/language first — to 7 stacks + generic floor; unknown => generic. (2) index.ts: memoised load() (module-level or instance cache), forStack/all, skillsRoot option (mirror stack-knowledge.ts:187 test seam); use node:fs/promises, never throw (missing file => []). (3) selector.ts: pure ranking — score = stack-match + keyword overlap (signature.keywords vs diffKeywords) + path hints; cap topK; always concat the generic-floor set (dedup). (4) resolver.ts: resolveStackSecurityContext replacing stack-knowledge.ts:185 — render signatures into a compact prompt fragment (id/title/invariant + unsafe/safe fenced) + optional threatModelText; NEVER empty (fallback to generic floor). (5) security-auditor-agent.ts: replace the ctx = await resolveStackSecurityContext(config.project.stack) call (line ~79) with the new input object; derive changedPaths/diffKeywords from contract.estimatedFiles for now (diff provider is sprint 6). Keep buildUserMessage folding the fragment in. Do NOT change the read-only toolset or the fail-closed parse. Update the existing security-auditor-agent tests where the resolver call shape changed.
evaluatorNotes: sc-5-1: table-test resolve() over all 7 keywords + an unknown => generic. sc-5-2: load the real files via a real skillsRoot pointing at the repo skills/ dir; assert each stack indexes >=6. sc-5-3: craft a diffKeywords set matching a known signature's keywords, assert it's selected and topK respected and generic floor present. sc-5-4: assert promptFragment non-empty for all 8 and contains a stack-appropriate token (G3: no head-excerpt frontmatter). sc-5-5: stub runAgenticLoop, capture the userMessage, assert a retrieved signature id/title appears in it. sc-5-6: deep-equal config-omitting-security parse; full suite + build + typecheck + lint. Verify NO Bash/Write/Edit added to the auditor toolset.
